title: Yacht Charter Fuengirola: Prices, Seasons & Tips for 2026
html:
Chartering a yacht in Fuengirola offers a stylish way to explore the Costa del Sol by sea. Departing from Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola, you can expect to pay from 250 EUR for a half-day skippered motorboat to over 1,500 EUR for a full-day crewed catamaran. BoatHire24 connects you with local owners so you can quickly find a skippered charter with verified availability.

Where to set off in Fuengirola

Most charters begin at Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola, a modern marina with full facilities and easy parking. Alternatively, you can set out from the nearby Puerto de la Torre in Mijas Costa or the larger Puerto José Banús in Marbella, 18 nautical miles west. Popular cruising grounds include the Bahía de Fuengirola, the coast toward Calahonda, and the sheltered waters off Cabo Pino. You can find a skippered charter in a few minutes.

Which boat to rent

  1. Speedboats, best for a short coastal blast; up to 6 guests; from 300 EUR half-day.
  2. Motor yachts, ideal for a comfortable family day out; up to 10 guests; from 600 EUR half-day.
  3. Sailing catamarans, perfect for stable cruising and sunbathing; up to 12 guests; from 900 EUR full-day.
  4. Luxury crewed yachts, for a premium experience with skipper and steward; up to 12 guests; from 1,500 EUR full-day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Fuengirola?

Prices range from 150 EUR for a shared sunset tour to over 2,000 EUR for a fully crewed luxury yacht. One option worth a look is the Olympic 490 for hire.

  • Shared tour (2 hours): from 150 EUR per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours, unskippered): from 250 EUR including fuel.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours, skippered): from 900 EUR.
  • Crewed motor yacht full-day (8 hours, inclusive): from 1,500 EUR.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Fuengirola?

For most small motorboats and speedboats in Spain, a valid ICC or equivalent is required; the operator confirms exact requirements for each boat. To see how booking works, visit the booking FAQ.

If you anchor off the beach in Bahía de Fuengirola, watch for the seasonal ‘posidonia’ seagrass meadows, please avoid dropping anchor on them to protect the marine environment.

The high season runs from June to September, when the Levante wind can pick up in the afternoon. Morning trips offer the calmest conditions.

At a glance

  • Price range: 250, 2,000+ EUR
  • Peak months: June, September
  • Trip length: 2, 8 hours
  • Max passengers: 12 (typically)
  • Currency accepted: EUR
  • Licence required: varies by boat; confirm with host

Questions about boat rental in Fuengirola

Can I rent a boat in Fuengirola without a licence?

Yes, you can hire skippered boats or small low-power vessels that do not demand a licence, as long as the owner provides a brief safety briefing.

What is the best time of year to charter a yacht in Fuengirola?

The ideal period is from May to October, when sea temperatures are pleasant and the Mediterranean is generally calm, though the Levante wind can pick up in July and August.

Can I bring my own food and drink on a charter?

Most private charters allow you to bring your own provisions, but some all-inclusive packages already include a selection of drinks and tapas, check the listing for details.

What is the cancellation policy for a boat rental in Fuengirola?

Cancellation terms vary by owner, but many offer free cancellation up to 48 hours before departure; you can review this on the booking page before confirming.

To check availability and compare prices for your chosen dates, visit the online marketplace. If you own a boat in Fuengirola, list your boat on BoatHire24 and start earning from the growing demand for coastal charters in 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Fuengirola and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
